stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \) and \( B = \begin{bmatrix} [[B11]] & [[B12]] \\ [[B21]] & [[B22]] \end{bmatrix} \). Let \( X \) be the \( 2\times2 \) matrix satisfying \( AX = B \). What is the entry in the first row and first column of \( X \)?
distractor_rationales: This is the \( (1,2) \) entry of \( X \), not the \( (1,1) \) entry.
This is the \( (2,1) \) entry of \( X \).
This is the \( (2,2) \) entry of \( X \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by one from the correct entry.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \) and \( B = \begin{bmatrix} [[p]] & [[q]] \\ [[r]] & [[s]] \end{bmatrix} \). Let \( X \) be the \( 2\times2 \) matrix satisfying \( XA = B \) (note \( A \) multiplies \( X \) on the RIGHT). What is the entry in the first row and first column of \( X \)?
solution: Since \( A \) is invertible and multiplies \( X \) on the right, \( X = BA^{-1} \), not \( A^{-1}B \) (matrix multiplication does not commute). The \( (1,1) \) entry of \( BA^{-1} \) is \( \frac{[[p]]\cdot[[d]] - [[q]]\cdot[[c]]}{([[a]])([[d]]) - ([[b]])([[c]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This wrongly computes \( A^{-1}B \) (left-multiplication) instead of \( BA^{-1} \) (right-multiplication).
This is the \( (1,2) \) entry of \( B \), not an entry of \( X \).
This is the \( (2,1) \) entry of \( B \), not an entry of \( X \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by one from the correct entry.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \), \( B = \begin{bmatrix} [[p]] & [[q]] \\ [[r]] & [[s]] \end{bmatrix} \), and \( C = \begin{bmatrix} [[w]] & [[x]] \\ [[y]] & [[z]] \end{bmatrix} \). Let \( X \) be the \( 2\times2 \) matrix satisfying \( AXB = C \). What is the entry in the first row and first column of \( X \)?
solution: Since \( A \) and \( B \) are both invertible, \( X = A^{-1}CB^{-1} \). Computing \( Y = CB^{-1} \) first gives \( Y_{11} = [[Y11]] \) and \( Y_{21} = [[Y21]] \); then the \( (1,1) \) entry of \( A^{-1}Y \) is \( \frac{[[d]]\cdot([[Y11]]) - [[b]]\cdot([[Y21]])}{[[detA]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the \( (1,1) \) entry of the intermediate matrix \( Y = CB^{-1} \), before left-multiplying by \( A^{-1} \).
This is the \( (1,1) \) entry of \( C \) itself, ignoring both matrix inversions.
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by two from the correct entry.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \), \( B = \begin{bmatrix} [[p]] & \ast \\ [[r]] & \ast \end{bmatrix} \), and \( C = \begin{bmatrix} [[w]] & \ast \\ [[y]] & \ast \end{bmatrix} \) (only the first columns of \( B \) and \( C \) matter below). Let \( X \) be the \( 2\times2 \) matrix satisfying \( AX + B = C \). What is the entry in the first row and first column of \( X \)?
distractor_rationales: This uses \( C \) alone in place of \( C-B \), forgetting to subtract \( B \) before inverting.
This is just the difference of the raw entries, without applying \( A^{-1} \) at all.
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by two from the correct entry.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]] \end{bmatrix} \), \( I \) be the \( 2\times2 \) identity matrix, and \( B = \begin{bmatrix} [[p]] & [[q]] \\ [[r]] & [[s]] \end{bmatrix} \). Let \( X \) be the \( 2\times2 \) matrix satisfying \( XA + X = B \). What is the entry in the first row and first column of \( X \)?
solution: Factoring out \( X \) on the left, \( XA + X = X(A+I) = B \), so \( X = B(A+I)^{-1} \), where \( A + I = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]]+1 & [[b]] \\ [[c]] & [[d]]+1 \end{bmatrix} \). The \( (1,1) \) entry of \( B(A+I)^{-1} \) is \( \frac{[[p]]\cdot([[d]]+1) - [[q]]\cdot[[c]]}{([[a]]+1)([[d]]+1) - ([[b]])([[c]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add the identity matrix, inverting \( A \) itself instead of \( A+I \).
This is the \( (1,2) \) entry of \( B \), not an entry of \( X \).
This is the \( (2,1) \) entry of \( B \), not an entry of \( X \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
This is off by one from the correct entry.
